model_context.source: User-supplied NBA official scorer report + Kalshi live screenshots

liveflow_read.pace_context: Q1 total 53 points with Cleveland producing elite perimeter efficiency and high assist conversion. Raw pace supports the pregame over, but Cleveland 6-of-13 from three is above sustainable baseline.
liveflow_read.cleveland_profile: Cleveland offense opened clean: 10 assists on 12 made field goals, only 1 turnover, strong ball movement, and bench shooting from Merrill/Wade immediately activated.
liveflow_read.detroit_profile: Detroit offense is below required team-total pace. Cade and Duren are involved, but Harris is cold offensively and team assist rate is weak at only 2 assists on 8 makes.
liveflow_read.rebound_watch: Tobias Harris has 2 rebounds after 7:27, on pace for 7+ if minutes hold near projection. Current read remains alive, not downgraded.

q2_watch_thresholds.over_205_5_safe_path: Need 50+ Q2 points or halftime total 101+ with no severe pace collapse.
q2_watch_thresholds.det_team_total_recovery_path: Detroit needs 29+ Q2 points to restore 104.5 team-total viability.
q2_watch_thresholds.harris_rebound_path: Harris needs 1 more rebound by halftime to stay cleanly on 7+ pace. Two more by half would upgrade to strong live hold.

liveflow_decision.reason: Pregame over is active and live total moved to 208.5. Detroit team total +118 looks tempting but model has DET O104.5 below fair threshold after weak Q1 creation. Avoid correlated add until Detroit confirms offensive recovery in Q2.
liveflow_decision.risk_cluster_status: No additional correlated over exposure recommended. Preserve $5 live bullet for halftime or Q3 entry.
